## FHIR Queries — Mesalamine (5-ASA) Prescriptions

Mesalamine (RxNorm ingredient: **41493**) is searched across prescription, dispensing, and self-reported medication resources, including Patient records.

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41493&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_include=MedicationRequest:medication

MedicationDispense?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41493&_include=MedicationDispense:patient&_include=MedicationDispense:medication

MedicationStatement?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41493&_include=MedicationStatement:patient&_include=MedicationStatement:medication
```

---

### 🔍 Query Rationale

| Resource | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `MedicationRequest` | Active/historical prescriptions ordered by a clinician |
| `MedicationDispense` | Medications actually dispensed (e.g., pharmacy fill records) |
| `MedicationStatement` | Patient-reported or imported medication histories |

### 💊 Key Code Reference

| System | Code | Description |
|---|---|---|
| RxNorm | `41493` | Mesalamine *(ingredient / 5-ASA)* |

### 📝 Notes
- The **`41493`** code matches the **ingredient-level** concept, which will retrieve mesalamine across all formulations and brands (e.g., **Asacol HD**, **Lialda**, **Pentasa**, **Delzicol**, **Canasa**, **Rowasa**).
- Add `&status=active` to narrow to **currently active** prescriptions only.
- Add `&_revinclude=Condition:subject` on Patient results to cross-reference with **Ulcerative Colitis** diagnoses (ICD-10: `K51.*`).

1-expert.raw_response:
## FHIR Query: Mesalamine (5-ASA) Prescriptions

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41493&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

---

### Query Breakdown

| Component | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Resource** | `MedicationRequest` |
| **Code System** | `http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm` |
| **RxNorm Code** | `41493` — Mesalamine (ingredient-level concept) |
| **`_include`** | Pulls linked `Patient` resources in the same bundle |

---

### Why the Ingredient-Level Code (`41493`)

Using the **ingredient/clinical drug component** RxNorm code casts the widest net, covering all formulations and routes relevant to IBD therapy:

| Formulation | Route | Common Brand |
|---|---|---|
| Delayed-release tablet | Oral | Asacol HD, Delzicol |
| Extended-release capsule | Oral | Apriso, Pentasa |
| Rectal suppository | Rectal | Canasa |
| Rectal enema | Rectal | Rowasa |
| Multi-matrix tablet | Oral | Lialda |

> ⚠️ **Specificity Caveat (as noted):** Mesalamine is used for both **Ulcerative Colitis** and **Crohn's Disease** (especially Crohn's colitis). This query captures the first-line UC agent but will include Crohn's patients. To refine to UC specifically, combine with a `Condition` query:

```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K51&_revinclude=MedicationRequest:patient
```

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41493&_has:Condition:patient:code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K51
```

> 💡 **ICD-10 Note:** `K51` is the UC parent code (covers K51.0–K51.9 subtypes). `K50` is Crohn's disease — excluding patients with *only* K50 and *no* K51 would further improve specificity.
